hero_title_accent: Secure Rollout for Banks and Auditors
hero_subtitle: SFTY supports banks and regulated institutions in deploying phased reporting workflows that deliver audit-ready evidence, role-based governance, and clear oversight across digital asset compliance.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Reporting workflows slow down without integration
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle with delays and gaps when reporting data is scattered across multiple systems and manual processes. This fragmentation weakens audit readiness and makes it harder to produce consistent, verifiable evidence for management, banks, and regulators.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects data from multiple sources into unified reporting workflows with role-based access, preserving audit evidence and enabling banks and auditors to produce consistent, verifiable compliance reports efficiently.
workflow_title: From evidence package to regulatory report
workflow_description: SFTY structures case evidence and decision logs into organized reporting packages ready for management, auditors, regulators and banking due diligence workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented compliance outputs into structured cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence that supports controlled reporting workflows for institutional trust and governance teams.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY provides banks with a controlled layer for phased reporting deployment, preserving audit-ready evidence and supporting secure governance across compliance and due diligence workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support phased reporting rollout for banks
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how reporting workflows connect audit-ready evidence
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how role-based access governs compliance data security
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how phased deployment improves operational compliance readiness
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for banks in compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY acts as a compliance control layer connecting KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and reporting workflows into one structured system for banks and financial institutions in regulated digital asset markets.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure reporting workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes alerts, provider results and regulatory obligations into connected cases, decision logs and reports suitable for management, auditors and regulatory submissions in a controlled workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and evidence packages to support audit readiness and regulatory inspection for compliance teams and enterprise governance.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects existing KYC, KYT, screening and Travel Rule providers into one operational platform without replacing them, allowing seamless integration with current compliance stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or KYT providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but rather sits above them, connecting their outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and audit-ready reports.
final_title: Manage phased reporting rollout with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY supports audit-ready evidence, role-based governance and clear oversight for institutional compliance reporting workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
